raw_consumer_statement:
Harassment, mishandled dispute, and retaliatory collection actions American Express has mishandled a billing dispute regarding a veterinary charge that was paid upfront and is not owed. Instead of properly investigating, they misclassified my dispute as a tort claim and repeatedly recycled denial letters. 

While the dispute remained active with regulators ( NY Attorney General and NY Department of Financial Services ), Amex reduced my credit limit, later restored it after I filed with CFPB on XX/XX/XXXX, and then suddenly closed my account, retroactively reporting it as closed since early XXXX, even though I did not receive the closure notice until later. And it wasnt reported as closed until XXXX.. It was all done behind the behind the curtains. 

American Express also deleted a previously reported late payment and most recently reported me as 90 days late on XX/XX/XXXX, raising serious concerns about inconsistent reporting and retaliation. Their responses cite only vague policies and procedures, without citing factual or regulatory justification, and they falsely claim no knowledge of my ongoing AG complaint in recent communications. 

While this dispute remains unresolved, American Express has now engaged XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX to contact me for collection. XXXX is not a new debt purchaser but is acting as Amexs agent. They are calling and leaving messages regarding a disputed balance that has never been properly validated, without sending the debt validation letter required under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. 

This harassment is causing me serious stress and aggravating existing medical conditions, including ongoing evaluatiXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX concerns. I am currently too ill to handle multiple creditor calls, yet Amex and its agents are proceeding as though I am liable for a fraudulent or mishandled charge. 

Amex is improperly using XXXX to pursue collection rather than fulfilling their obligation to investigate the dispute fully. This behavior is unlawful and retaliatory, and it is directly damaging my credit and my health.
